  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Drain slope is less than the required 1/4 inch per foot for standard residential drainage piping (2 inches or less in diameter)."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-hang or re-route the drain line to establish a consistent slope of at least 1/4 inch per foot before closing up walls or ceilings."
  ],
  "rationale": "According to IPC Section 704, gravity drainage piping must be installed in alignment and at a continuous slope. For pipe sizes 2 inches or less, the minimum required slope is 1/4 inch per foot. Slopes less than this threshold fail to maintain the velocity required to transport solids, leading to blockages.",
  "references": [
    "IPC 704.1"
  ]
}
